channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fadnavis Denies Reports of Midnight Meeting with Uddhav Thackeray in Mumbai
item.description: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis has categorically denied reports suggesting that he held a secret meeting with Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray. The speculation arose after a media ...

item.title: Youtuber Salim Wastik Arrested After 31 Years for 1995 Kidnap and Murder
item.description: Salim Khan, known as Salim Wastik, a man wanted for the 1995 kidnap and murder of a 13-year-old boy in Delhi, has been arrested after evading authorities for 31 years. He had assumed a new identity an...

item.title: Njugush Reveals Why He Took Time to File for Divorce, Talks About ‘The Boys’
item.description: Kenyan comedian Njugush has recently confirmed his separation from his long-time partner, Celestine Ndinda, also known as Wakavinye, after 14 years of marriage. This revelation has sparked much discus...

item.title: EFCC Warns Creators Against Unauthorized Use of Its Identity in Online Content
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) don raise serious warning give content creators wey dey use the agency name, logo, uniform, and other official identity for skits and online videos ...

item.title: Researchers Discover Hidden Voids in Menkaure Pyramid, Possibly Pointing to Secret Entrance
item.description: A new discovery in the Menkaure pyramid has raised intriguing questions about its structure. Using non-invasive scanning techniques like radar, ultrasound, and electrical resistivity tomography, resea...

item.title: Calls for Transparency in Funding of First Lady’s Initiatives in Nigeria
item.description: Dr. Saidu Ahmad Dukawa, Associate Professor of Public Administration at Bayero University Kano, don raise concern say the Renewed Hope Initiative, wey First Lady dey lead, don become partisan and e de...

item.title: Manguzi Community on Edge After Death of Suspected Murderer Linked to Traditional Healer
item.description: The community of KwaMhlabuyalingana, KwaZulu-Natal, is gripped by fear and tension following the death of Sifiso “Coach” Malwane, a suspect in the murder of renowned traditional healer James ‘Godo’ Mt...

item.title: Rising violence and structural pressures driving deadly attacks in UK prisons
item.description: Recent incidents in UK prisons, including the deaths of Ian Huntley and Ian Watkins, have drawn renewed attention to escalating violence behind bars. Ministry of Justice figures show serious assaults ...

item.title: Seven people die in Ukraine amid Russian drone strike; RAF positions Typhoon jets in Romania
item.description: A significant drone attack by Russian forces in Ukraine has resulted in the deaths of seven individuals, highlighting the ongoing severity of the conflict in the region. The attack represents a contin...

item.title: Carolyne Waithera Opens Up About New Relationship After Announcing Engagement
item.description: Carolyne Waithera, the widow of the late Kikuyu musician John DeMathew, has given her followers a glimpse of her new partner after sharing the news of her engagement six years following her husband's ...

item.title: APGA Announces 30 April Deadline for Membership Revalidation Before 2027 Elections
item.description: The All Progressives Grand Alliance (APGA) has set 30 April 2026 as the final deadline for both new and existing members to complete their membership revalidation and registration ahead of the 2027 ge...

item.title: Meghann Fahy Discusses Career Highlights, Personal Challenges and Life Lessons
item.description: Meghann Fahy, the American actress known for her roles on Broadway and television, opens up about personal and professional experiences in a candid interview. Born in Massachusetts, Fahy began her car...

item.title: Quezon City police vehicle driver to face charges after wall collision
item.description: Authorities in Quezon City have confirmed that the driver of a police mobile involved in an accident will face legal charges. The vehicle reportedly collided with a wall while transporting detainees, ...

item.title: Carrie Tait recognized for investigative journalism by the National Newspaper Association
item.description: Carrie Tait of The Globe and Mail has been honoured as Journalist of the Year by the National Newspaper Association (NNA) for her determined investigative reporting on a political spending scandal in ...

item.title: Trump's Indictment of SPLC: Legal Battle and Its Impact on Civil Rights Groups
item.description: The Trump administration’s indictment of the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) has sparked concerns about its long-term effects on civil rights organizations. Accused of funneling over $3 million to ...

item.title: Parliamentary committee proposes stricter conflict-of-interest rules for prime minister, unlikely to pass
item.description: A House of Commons ethics committee has released a report recommending significant changes to Canada’s Conflict of Interest Act, with several proposals aimed directly at Prime Minister Mark Carney’s f...

item.title: Ombudsman seeks travel restriction on Escudero and Ngu over alleged flood control fund anomalies
item.description: Hiniling ng Office of the Ombudsman sa Sandiganbayan ang paglabas ng isang precautionary hold departure order laban kay Senator Francis “Chiz” Escudero at negosyanteng si Maynard Ngu habang iniimbesti...

item.title: Opposition MPs file fresh motion for removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar
item.description: On 25th April 2026, opposition MPs in the Rajya Sabha submitted a fresh notice to the RS Secretary General, seeking to initiate a motion for the removal of Gyanesh Kumar from his position as Chief Ele...

item.title: High Court emphasises need for expert testimony in rape case prosecutions
item.description: The Pietermaritzburg High Court has issued a stern reminder to prosecutors about the importance of calling medical experts to testify in sexual assault cases, rather than relying solely on written med...

item.title: Australian Women and Children Repatriated from Syrian Detention Camp
item.description: Four Australian women and nine of their children and grandchildren have been repatriated from the al-Roj detention camp in north-east Syria, marking a significant, albeit partial, resolution to a long...
